tasks.title: Rename VIP_PROMOTIONS_ACCESS to VIP_VISIBILITY_ACCESS across all layers
tasks.description:
Pure refactor (D-5). Rename the entitlement key everywhere — behavior of the 8 visibility checks stays identical.

Files to change:
- packages/billing/src/types/entitlement.types.ts: rename enum member VIP_PROMOTIONS_ACCESS → VIP_VISIBILITY_ACCESS; update the string value from 'vip_promotions_access' to 'vip_visibility_access'.
- packages/billing/src/config/entitlements.config.ts: rename key reference + update name/description metadata.
- packages/billing/src/config/plans.config.ts: rename in TOURIST_VIP_ENTITLEMENTS array (line ~60).
- packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.service.ts: 6 string checks at lines 2110, 2155, 2212, 2285, 2565, 2630.
- packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.permissions.ts: 1 string check at line 185.
- apps/admin/src/features/billing-plans/components/plan-entitlement-groups.ts: rename EntitlementKey reference (line ~61).
- packages/i18n/src/types.ts: rename the i18n key literal 'billing.entitlement.vip_promotions_access' → 'billing.entitlement.vip_visibility_access' (line ~5249).
- packages/billing/test/entitlements.test.ts: update hardcoded string at line ~176.
- packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/accommodation.activeOnly.test.ts: update strings at lines ~100, ~218.
- packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/getByDestination.test.ts: update strings at lines ~9, ~153, ~159.
- apps/api/test/middlewares/entitlement-plan.cutover.test.ts: update hardcoded 'vip_promotions_access' at line ~75.

DB extras migration (next available number in packages/db/src/migrations/extras/):
- UPDATE billing_entitlements SET key = 'vip_visibility_access' WHERE key = 'vip_promotions_access';
- UPDATE billing_plans SET entitlements = (SELECT jsonb_agg(CASE WHEN elem = '"vip_promotions_access"' THEN '"vip_visibility_access"' ELSE elem END) FROM jsonb_array_elements(entitlements) elem) WHERE entitlements @> '["vip_promotions_access"]';
- UPDATE billing_customer_entitlements SET entitlement_key = 'vip_visibility_access' WHERE entitlement_key = 'vip_promotions_access' (if table/column exists).

Mandatory regression test: add a test in packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/ proving that a VIP actor (entitlements: new Set(['vip_visibility_access'])) still bypasses the visibility filters (RESTRICTED / planRestricted / ownerSuspended) in accommodation.service.ts. This test MUST be written BEFORE the rename to confirm it fails, then passes after — verifying no silent breakage in the 8 call sites.

Acceptance criteria:
- TypeScript compiles clean (no references to old key).
- All existing accommodation-visibility tests pass unchanged.
- Regression test proves VIP visibility perk works with the new key string.
- Extras migration runs idempotently (SQL is safe to re-apply).
- No behavior change to what VIP actors can see.

Update all test files with hardcoded old string
Write idempotent extras SQL migration for DB rename
Verify regression test GREEN + full typecheck pass

tasks.title: Create tourist_price_alerts DB table, structural migration, and BaseModel
tasks.description:
Structural carril (pnpm db:generate → migration 0037_* in packages/db/src/migrations/).

Create packages/db/src/schemas/alert/tourist_price_alerts.dbschema.ts:
- Table tourist_price_alerts with columns:
  - id: uuid PK (default gen_random_uuid())
  - user_id: uuid NOT NULL FK → users.id ON DELETE CASCADE
  - accommodation_id: uuid NOT NULL FK → accommodations.id ON DELETE CASCADE
  - base_price_snapshot: integer NOT NULL (price in centavos at subscription time; used for % drop comparison)
  - target_percent_drop: integer NULL (null = alert on any drop; 1-100 range enforced at schema level)
  - is_active: boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT true
  - created_at, updated_at (with timezone), deleted_at (soft delete)
- Unique constraint: UNIQUE(user_id, accommodation_id) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL (only one active alert per user+accommodation pair)
- Indexes:
  - tourist_price_alerts_userId_idx on user_id
  - tourist_price_alerts_accommodationId_idx on accommodation_id
  - tourist_price_alerts_isActive_idx on is_active (for cron batch query)

Run pnpm db:generate to produce the migration SQL, commit the generated file alongside the schema change.

Create packages/db/src/models/alert/touristPriceAlert.model.ts:
- Extends BaseModel
- Export from packages/db/src/models/index.ts

Add relation: users → many(touristPriceAlerts), accommodations → many(touristPriceAlerts) in the respective dbschema relation definitions.

Acceptance criteria:
- pnpm db:generate produces a non-empty migration.
- TypeScript compiles clean.
- Model extends BaseModel and is exported from the models index.
- Unique constraint present in the generated SQL.

tasks.title: Implement AlertSubscriptionService in @repo/service-core
tasks.description:
Create packages/service-core/src/services/alert/alert-subscription.service.ts extending BaseCrudService for the tourist_price_alerts table.

Methods (all RO-RO, return Result<T>):
- create({ actor, input: CreatePriceAlertInput }): validates accommodation exists; reads current accommodation.price.price to populate base_price_snapshot; checks for duplicate subscription (UNIQUE constraint — handle CONFLICT_ERROR); returns created PriceAlert.
- remove({ actor, alertId }): loads alert, validates actor owns it (actor.id === alert.userId), soft-deletes via deletedAt.
- list({ actor, input: ListPriceAlertsInput }): returns paginated list of active alerts for actor.id; includes accommodation slug/name snapshot for display.
- countActive({ actor }): returns integer count of non-deleted alerts for actor.id (used by gateAlerts() context pre-population before calling the middleware).

Permission model:
- create/remove: actor must be authenticated (no special permission beyond that; ownership enforced in service).
- list/countActive: actor must match userId (no admin list in v1).
- Staff bypass: SUPER_ADMIN and ADMIN can call on behalf of any user (use PermissionEnum.ACCOMMODATION_VIEW_ALL as the staff bypass check, consistent with other tourist services).

Accomodation price extraction: read accommodation.price JSONB; parse as AccommodationPriceSchema; extract price.price (integer centavos). If price is null/missing, reject creation with SERVICE_ERROR_INVALID_INPUT.

Export from packages/service-core/src/services/index.ts.

Unit tests in packages/service-core/test/services/alert/alert-subscription.service.test.ts:
- create: success, accommodation not found, duplicate subscription.
- remove: success, not-found alert, wrong actor.
- list: returns paginated active alerts, excludes soft-deleted.
- countActive: returns correct count.
- Confirm SPEC-216 owner-inherits-tourist pattern: an owner actor can also create alerts.

Acceptance criteria:
- Service compiles clean.
- All unit tests pass with >= 90% coverage on new service code.

subtasks.title: Implement create() with accommodation price snapshot extraction
Implement remove() with ownership check
Implement list() + countActive()
Export from service-core services index
Write unit tests (create/remove/list/countActive/duplicate/owner-bypass)

tasks.title: Mount /protected/price-alerts API routes with gateAlerts() gate
tasks.description:
Create thin Hono routes following the three-tier API architecture. All routes live at /api/v1/protected/price-alerts.

Files to create in apps/api/src/routes/price-alert/protected/:
- create.ts: POST / — pre-populate currentActiveAlertsCount via AlertSubscriptionService.countActive(actor) (set on context as c.set('currentActiveAlertsCount', count)), then gateAlerts() middleware from tourist-entitlements.ts, then call AlertSubscriptionService.create(). Use createSimpleRoute/createOpenApiRoute factory. Return 201 + created alert.
- remove.ts: DELETE /:alertId — gateAlerts() not needed for deletion; validate alertId as UUID; call AlertSubscriptionService.remove(). Return 204.
- list.ts: GET / — no gate (listing doesn't consume the limit); call AlertSubscriptionService.list(). Return 200 + paginated list.
- index.ts: Hono sub-app mounting all three.

Register in apps/api/src/routes/index.ts:
- Import { priceAlertProtectedRoutes } from './price-alert/index.js';
- Mount at /protected/price-alerts inside the PROTECTED ROUTES block.

Update docs/billing/endpoint-gate-matrix.md:
- Add rows for POST /protected/price-alerts (gate: PRICE_ALERTS entitlement + MAX_ACTIVE_ALERTS limit) and GET/DELETE /protected/price-alerts/:id (no gate).

Acceptance criteria:
- POST /protected/price-alerts returns 403 ENTITLEMENT_REQUIRED for a free-tier tourist.
- POST /protected/price-alerts returns 403 LIMIT_REACHED when at MAX_ACTIVE_ALERTS.
- POST /protected/price-alerts returns 201 for an entitled tourist.
- DELETE and GET return 200/204 for authenticated actors.
- TypeScript compiles clean.

tasks.title: Implement PriceDropEvaluator service
tasks.description:
Create packages/service-core/src/services/alert/price-drop-evaluator.service.ts.

Purpose: given a time window, find all tourist_price_alerts whose subscribed accommodation has dropped in price past the alert's threshold.

Interface (RO-RO):
- evaluatePriceDrops({ since: Date }): Promise<Map<string, PriceDropMatch[]>>
  - Queries active tourist_price_alerts (is_active = true, deleted_at IS NULL).
  - For each alert, reads the accommodation's current accommodation.price.price (integer centavos).
  - Drop % = (base_price_snapshot - current_price) / base_price_snapshot * 100.
  - If drop > 0 AND drop >= (alert.targetPercentDrop ?? globalDefaultThresholdPercent):
    - Include in results map keyed by userId.
  - globalDefaultThresholdPercent: read from env var HOSPEDA_ALERT_PRICE_DROP_THRESHOLD_PCT (default 5). Register in packages/config/src/env-registry.*.ts + apps/api/src/utils/env.ts.
  - Filters to alerts whose accommodation was updated within the `since` window (joins on accommodations.updated_at >= since) to avoid evaluating unchanged prices.

Types to export:
- PriceDropMatch: { alertId, userId, accommodationId, accommodationSlug, accommodationName, basePriceSnapshot, currentPrice, dropPercent, currency }

Unit tests in packages/service-core/test/services/alert/price-drop-evaluator.service.test.ts:
- Price dropped past threshold: included.
- Price dropped but below threshold: excluded.
- Price increased: excluded (drop is negative).
- Price unchanged: excluded.
- null targetPercentDrop + dropped: uses global default threshold.
- Accommodation updated_at before window: excluded.

Acceptance criteria:
- All unit tests pass.
- No any types.
- Evaluation logic is a pure computation — no side effects, easily testable with mock data.

subtasks.title: Implement evaluatePriceDrops() with threshold logic
Register HOSPEDA_ALERT_PRICE_DROP_THRESHOLD_PCT env var in @repo/config + apps/api env.ts
Export PriceDropMatch type + service from service-core index
Write unit tests covering all threshold edge cases

tasks.title: Create alerts-digest daily cron job
tasks.description:
Create apps/api/src/cron/jobs/alerts-digest.job.ts following the established cron pattern (mirror trial-expiry.ts or conversation-notification.job.ts).

Schedule: '0 8 * * *' (8 AM daily).

Job flow:
1. Call PriceDropEvaluator.evaluatePriceDrops({ since: yesterday_8AM }), getting Map<userId, PriceDropMatch[]>.
2. Call PromoOfferEvaluator.evaluatePromoOffers({ since: yesterday_8AM }) (from T-012) — stub a no-op version until T-012 ships, wired via optional injection.
3. Merge results into a combined Map<userId, AlertDigestPayload> (price drops + promo offers per user).
4. For each userId with non-empty payload:
   a. Load user email from DB.
   b. Call AlertDigestDeliveryService.deliver(payload) from @repo/notifications (T-008).
   c. Log success/failure; continue on per-user error (no early exit).
5. Batch users in groups of 50 to avoid memory pressure (consistent with dunning.job.ts pattern).
6. Return { processed, errors, durationMs } (consistent with CronJobDefinition contract).

Register in apps/api/src/cron/jobs/index.ts export list.
Register schedule in apps/api/src/cron/cron-registry.ts (or equivalent scheduler bootstrap).

Context/DI pattern: inject AlertDigestDeliveryService and evaluators via constructor params or module-level singletons — mirror how trial-expiry.ts uses TrialService and sendNotification.

Dry-run support: when ctx.dryRun = true, skip actual delivery calls, log what would have been sent.

Acceptance criteria:
- CronJobDefinition shape satisfied (name, description, schedule, enabled, handler).
- Dry-run path tested in unit tests.
- Error in one user's delivery does not abort the batch.
- Job is registered in the cron registry.

subtasks.title: Scaffold alerts-digest.job.ts with CronJobDefinition shape
Implement price-drop batch + user merge logic
Wire AlertDigestDeliveryService.deliver() call
Register in cron index + scheduler
Write unit test for dry-run path + per-user error isolation

tasks.title: Channel-agnostic delivery abstraction in @repo/notifications
tasks.description:
Build the extensible delivery layer that accepts future WhatsApp and push transports without touching the subscription or evaluation core.

Create packages/notifications/src/services/alert-delivery.service.ts:
- NotificationChannel interface: { readonly name: string; deliver(payload: AlertDigestPayload): Promise<void> }
- AlertDigestDeliveryService class:
  - Constructor accepts readonly channels: NotificationChannel[] (injectable; defaults to [emailChannel] in production)
  - deliver({ userId, userEmail, priceDrop: PriceDropMatch[], promoOffers: PromoOfferMatch[] }): iterates channels, calls channel.deliver(), swallows per-channel errors with structured logging
  - deliverBatch(payloads: AlertDigestPayload[]): iterates payloads calling deliver()

Create packages/notifications/src/types/alert.types.ts:
- AlertDigestPayload: { userId, userEmail, locale, priceDrop: PriceDropMatch[], promoOffers: PromoOfferMatch[] }
- PromoOfferMatch: { promotionId, accommodationId, accommodationName, accommodationSlug, promotionTitle, discountType, discountValue, validUntil: Date | null }
  (note: PriceDropMatch is imported from service-core via cross-package import or re-declared here; prefer re-declaration to avoid circular deps)
- TriggerKind: 'price_drop' | 'promo_offer' as const

Create packages/notifications/src/services/channels/email-alert.channel.ts:
- Implements NotificationChannel
- Renders AlertDigestEmail (from T-009) via React Email + sends via existing EmailTransport
- If both priceDrop and promoOffers are empty, skips send silently

Export all from packages/notifications/src/services/index.ts and the package root index.

Unit tests in packages/notifications/test/services/alert-delivery.service.test.ts:
- deliver() calls all injected channels.
- Per-channel error does not abort other channels.
- Empty payload does not call any channel.

Acceptance criteria:
- No @repo/service-core circular dependency (keep type definitions local).
- Interface exported so future WhatsApp/push channels can implement it without touching AlertDigestDeliveryService.
- Unit tests pass.

subtasks.title: Create alert.types.ts (AlertDigestPayload, PromoOfferMatch, TriggerKind)

Create email-alert.channel.ts (EmailAlertChannel implementing NotificationChannel)

tasks.title: Web alert management page (/cuenta/alertas)
tasks.description:
Build the tourist-facing alert management page in apps/web. This is an Astro page (SSR, no Tailwind).

Create apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/cuenta/alertas.astro (or /cuenta/alertas/index.astro matching the existing /cuenta/* routing pattern):
- Server-side fetch from GET /api/v1/protected/price-alerts using the user's session cookie.
- Render a list of active price-alert subscriptions.
- Each item shows: accommodation name (linked to detail page), current price, price at subscription time, target-drop percentage (or 'any drop').
- Delete form per alert (native HTML form → POST to /_actions/delete-alert or DELETE route via fetch island).
- Empty state: 'No tenés alertas activas. Explorá alojamientos y activá alertas de precio.' with CTA to /alojamientos.
- Plan-gate: if user is on free plan (PRICE_ALERTS entitlement absent), show upgrade CTA instead of the list.

Create supporting components:
- apps/web/src/components/alerts/AlertList.astro: renders the list.
- apps/web/src/components/alerts/AlertItem.astro: single alert row with delete form.
- apps/web/src/components/alerts/AlertEmptyState.astro: empty / plan-gate state.

Styling: CSS modules (*.module.css colocated with each component). No Tailwind.

Add i18n keys under account.alerts.* in packages/i18n (es/en/pt): page title, empty state text, delete CTA, column headers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Page renders server-side; authenticated user sees their alerts.
- Unauthenticated: redirect to /login (consistent with other /cuenta/* pages).
- Free-plan user: sees upgrade CTA, not the list.
- Delete removes the alert and refreshes.
- astro check passes on the new page.

tasks.title: Add 'Alert me for price drops' button on accommodation detail page
tasks.description:
Add a price-alert subscription toggle to apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/alojamientos/[slug].astro.

Create apps/web/src/components/accommodation/PriceAlertButton.tsx (React island, client:idle):
- Props: { accommodationId, userHasAlert: boolean, canCreateAlerts: boolean, maxReached: boolean }
- If canCreateAlerts = false (free plan): render a locked-state button that opens a plan-upgrade modal or links to /billing/plans. No API call.
- If maxReached = true (MAX_ACTIVE_ALERTS reached): render a disabled button with 'Límite de alertas alcanzado' tooltip.
- If canCreateAlerts = true and no existing alert: 'Avisame si baja el precio' button → POST /protected/price-alerts → optimistic toggle to active state.
- If active alert exists: 'Cancelar alerta' button → DELETE /protected/price-alerts/:alertId → toggle off.
- Show loading spinner during API call.
- Error state: inline error message (do not crash).

Wire into the detail page ([slug].astro):
- In the server-side Promise.allSettled block: fetch user's existing alert for this accommodation (GET /protected/price-alerts?accommodationId=X) and user entitlement state.
- Pass computed props (userHasAlert, canCreateAlerts, maxReached) to the island.
- Graceful degradation: if fetch fails, assume no alert + canCreateAlerts = false (safe default).

CSS modules for button styling (not Tailwind).

Acceptance criteria:
- Free-plan tourist sees a locked/upgrade CTA.
- Plus/VIP tourist can toggle the alert subscription.
- Existing alert correctly pre-populates the toggled state from SSR.
- No JavaScript error when user is unauthenticated (server should not render the button for guests at all).

tasks.title: PromoOfferEvaluator service + wire into digest cron + close SPEC-285 TODO stub
tasks.description:
Completes G-2. Depends on SPEC-285 being merged to staging (the OwnerPromotionLifecycleEvent + emitOwnerPromotionLifecycleEvent are already shipped by SPEC-285 T-004).

Create packages/service-core/src/services/alert/promo-offer-evaluator.service.ts:
- evaluatePromoOffers({ since: Date }): Promise<Map<string, PromoOfferMatch[]>>
  - Queries owner_promotions where (created_at >= since OR activated_at >= since) AND lifecycle_state = ACTIVE AND (validUntil IS NULL OR validUntil >= now()).
  - For each qualifying promotion, finds tourist_price_alerts subscribed to that promotion's accommodation_id OR (accommodation_id IS NULL and owner_id matches).
  - Returns Map<userId, PromoOfferMatch[]> (PromoOfferMatch type from T-008).
- Unit tests: promo created in window → matched subscribers returned; promo outside window → excluded; expired promo → excluded.

Update apps/api/src/cron/jobs/alerts-digest.job.ts (created in T-007):
- Replace the no-op PromoOfferEvaluator stub with the real evaluatePromoOffers call.
- Merge promo offer results into the user digest payload alongside price drops.

Close the SPEC-285 TODO stub in packages/service-core/src/services/owner-promotion/ownerPromotion.lifecycle-events.ts:
- Replace the // TODO(SPEC-286) comment block with a reference: add a comment noting that G-2 is handled by the daily-digest cron (alerts-digest.job.ts) rather than immediate event-driven delivery (matches D-3 daily-digest cadence decision).
- No code change needed in the emitter itself since the cron queries owner_promotions directly.

Export PromoOfferEvaluator from packages/service-core/src/services/index.ts.

Acceptance criteria:
- evaluatePromoOffers correctly matches subscribed tourists to qualifying promos.
- Cron digest includes promo-offer items alongside price-drop items.
- TODO(SPEC-286) stub comment is resolved (replaced with accurate cross-reference).
- Unit tests for PromoOfferEvaluator pass.
- SPEC-285 must be merged to staging before this task can be deployed (code can be written against SPEC-285's public API).
